description: Fully remove Owner Scope from Pibo's active product model, schemas, APIs, CLI/TUI, automation, workflows, tests, and current docs. The Ralph loop must work and validate only inside its Docker worker using isolated test databases; Production data migration and PR creation are final manual review-gated steps, not autonomous loop actions.

userStories.title: Establish final owner-scope removal baseline and progress tracking
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want an implementation baseline and progress record so that the Ralph loop can remove Owner Scope across the app without losing traceability.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md with branch, worktree, Docker worker, PRD paths, current status, and story progress
Link the plan, PRD, inventory summary, and raw inventory files

Record baseline rg counts for ownerScope, owner_scope, OwnerScope, owner-scope, shared:app, principalId, principal_id, room_members, listOwned, getOwned, requireOwned, personal target, active owner, and --owner-scope
Record read-only SQLite schema/column inventory for reachable affected stores without mutating data
Record Docker-only execution boundaries: runtime, deploy, gateway restart, CLI data commands, and DB mutation must use the Docker worker and isolated PIBO_HOME only
Create or verify a fresh ownerless Docker test PIBO_HOME separate from the copied migration sandbox
Run baseline npm run typecheck and npm run build, and record results
Do not change runtime behavior in this story
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01 in Docker worker pibo-dev-final-owner-scope-removal-ralph. Added docs/reports/final-owner-scope-removal-baseline-2026-06-01.md with baseline rg-compatible counts using node_modules/@vscode/ripgrep-linux-x64/bin/rg -F, active source/docs scope counts, and full worktree counts. Read-only SQLite schema inventory used Python sqlite3 URI mode=ro against the copied migration sandbox; fresh test home /workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home was verified separate and empty of SQLite DBs. Validation passed inside Docker with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run typecheck and npm run build. No runtime behavior changes; host /root/.pibo and host gateways were not touched.

userStories.title: Add strict owner-scope search gates with temporary allowlist
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated gates for Owner Scope vocabulary so that new work cannot reintroduce product ownership artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test or script that fails on active product matches for ownerScope, owner_scope, OwnerScope, owner-scope, shared:app, principalId, principal_id, room_members, listOwned, getOwned, and requireOwned
Support a narrow temporary allowlist for the final migration module and historical docs only
Document how to run the gate and how to shrink the allowlist to zero after cutover
Add focused tests for the gate behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Added npm script check:product-vocab backed by scripts/legacy-product-vocabulary-gate.mjs. The gate scans active product roots (src, packages, scripts, skills, test, docs/project, docs/specs, docs/plans) for the US-002 required terms and the broader final-removal vocabulary. The built-in temporary allowlist is limited to docs/legacy/** and the isolated final app-space cutover migration path. Documented usage and post-cutover allowlist shrink policy in docs/reports/final-owner-scope-search-gate-us-002.md and script --help. Added focused tests in test/legacy-product-vocabulary-gate.test.mjs for clean pass, active failures, multiple required spellings, historical-doc allowlisting, current-doc rejection, and isolated cutover migration allowlisting. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: node --test test/legacy-product-vocabulary-gate.test.mjs and npm run typecheck. A Docker scan summary of the current branch reported 3703 expected failures and 0 allowed matches across 1019 files because later removal stories have not yet removed active Owner Scope artifacts; this is not wired into npm test yet.

userStories.title: Replace shared-app legacy owner helpers with neutral app context
userStories.description: As an allowed Pibo user, I want the shared app context to be represented without a synthetic Owner Scope so that shared:app is not a product owner value.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove LEGACY_SHARED_APP_OWNER_SCOPE and getSharedAppLegacyOwnerScope from active runtime code
Keep a neutral app context representation that has no legacyOwnerScope or ownerScope field
Update imports and callers to stop requesting a replacement owner value
Focused tests cover neutral app context creation and absence of legacy owner fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Replaced src/shared-app.ts with a neutral app-context-only module: PiboSharedAppContext now has only kind and id, SHARED_APP_CONTEXT has no legacyOwnerScope/ownerScope, and the removed exports LEGACY_SHARED_APP_OWNER_SCOPE/getSharedAppLegacyOwnerScope no longer exist in src/packages/scripts/test. Existing pre-cutover schemas still require temporary owner_scope/principal_id write values until later schema-removal stories, so unavoidable legacy storage compatibility was isolated in src/owner-scope-compat.ts with explicit deletion guidance; later stories must remove those callers rather than treating it as product context. Updated source imports away from shared-app legacy helpers, updated tests to stop importing legacy owner symbols from shared-app, and updated web-auth focused tests to prove different auth identities share the same neutral appContext with no legacy owner fields. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run typecheck; npm run build; node --test test/web-auth-shared-app-context.test.mjs; node --test test/context-build-inspector.test.mjs test/cron-store-lifecycle.test.mjs test/session-router-store.test.mjs; and rg gate for getSharedAppLegacyOwnerScope|LEGACY_SHARED_APP_OWNER_SCOPE over src packages scripts test returned no matches. Host /root/.pibo and host gateways were not touched.

userStories.title: Make web auth an access gate without product owner fields
userStories.description: As an allowed web user, I want login to grant app access without creating product ownership state so that two accounts enter the same app context.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove ownerScope from PiboWebSession and related web auth/session types
Keep unauthenticated 401 behavior where auth is required
Ensure authenticated handlers receive auth identity only for access/display/audit, not visibility or write location
Add tests proving two allowed auth identities resolve to the same app context and no owner field
Focused web auth tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Removed ownerScope from PiboWebSession in src/web/types.ts and stopped requireWebSession from constructing a product owner field; it now returns only authSession plus the neutral appContext. Updated Chat Web and Context Files handlers that previously read webSession.ownerScope: unavoidable pre-cutover owner_scope storage compatibility now calls legacyOwnerScopeForPreCutoverSchemas() directly at legacy schema boundaries, while Context Files revision/change actor ids use the auth identity only as audit metadata. Focused web auth tests now assert unauthenticated requests still reject with PiboAuthError 401 and two authenticated identities receive the same ownerless app context with no ownerScope/legacyOwnerScope on either the web session or appContext. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run typecheck; npm run build; node --test test/web-auth-shared-app-context.test.mjs test/context-files-web.test.mjs; and grep over src/web src/apps/chat src/plugins plus the focused test found no webSession.ownerScope/PiboWebSession ownerScope call sites. Host /root/.pibo and host gateways were not touched.

userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Removed ownerScope from PiboSession, CreatePiboSessionInput, UpdatePiboSessionInput, and FindPiboSessionsInput in src/sessions/store.ts. userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Updated fresh SqlitePiboSessionStore pibo_sessions schema to omit owner_scope and idx_pibo_sessions_owner, and added constructor-time table rebuild for historical pibo_sessions tables that still contain owner_scope. The rebuild copies id, pi_session_id, channel, kind, profile, parent_id, origin_id, workspace, title, metadata_json, active_model_json when present, created_at, and updated_at into an ownerless table, then drops the legacy owner index/table. PiboDataSessionStore and pibo data migrate sessions-to-v2 no longer write owner_scope values even when importing historical pibo-sessions rows. Added temp-file fixture coverage for shared:app and user:* historical rows migrating to ownerless SqlitePiboSessionStore and pibo.sqlite sessions rows while preserving ids, piSessionIds, hierarchy/origin, metadata, active model, profile, workspace, titles, and timestamps. Added schema assertions that fresh pibo-sessions.sqlite and pibo.sqlite sessions tables contain no owner_scope column or owner index. A targeted Docker rg gate over src/sessions/sqlite-store.ts src/sessions/pibo-data-store.ts src/data/cli.ts for fresh owner column/index creation patterns returned no matches. Migration fixtures used only temp files in the Docker worker; host /root/.pibo and host gateways were not touched. Remaining src/data/cli.ts owner_scope references are in unread-baseline repair/read-state code scheduled for Chat read-state stories, not the pibo-sessions fresh schema.

userStories.title: Make Chat rooms, navigation, and read-state ownerless
userStories.description: As a Chat Web user, I want one shared room and navigation state so that no sidebar data is hidden by Owner Scope or Principal state.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove owner/principal parameters from room service, navigation store, session navigation, and read-state service APIs
Remove room membership as an access-control check in active behavior

userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Removed owner/principal parameters from active Chat room, navigation, and read-state service contracts: ChatRoomService listRooms/listRoomTree/ensureDefaultRoom/requireRoom/getMember/updateReadCursor are resource-id/app-level APIs; NavigationStore upsert/list inputs no longer accept ownerScope or filter by owner; ChatReadStateService writes and reads only app_session_read_state. Chat Web bootstrap/navigation/SSE/read endpoints now mark read state once per app session instead of per principal, active event-stream tracking no longer stores principals, and room access checks are resource existence plus archived write guard rather than room_members membership. Cron/Ralph Chat API room-target validation was updated to use requireRoom/ensureDefaultRoom without owner/principal room-service parameters. Focused tests updated for app-level unread counts and ownerless room-service calls. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run typecheck; npm run build; node --test test/chat-v2-native-services.test.mjs test/chat-cron-api.test.mjs test/ralph-resource-visibility.test.mjs; node --test test/chat-api-routes.test.mjs test/chat-web-shared-sessions.test.mjs. Host /root/.pibo, host Dev/Production gateways, and Production data were not touched. Remaining room ownerScope/principalId payload fields and fresh schema drops are left for US-009/US-010 as planned.

Drop active creation/use of room_members, principal_session_stats, and principal_room_stats in final schemas

userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Fresh pibo.sqlite Chat data schema already creates ownerless rooms, session_navigation, app_session_read_state, and app_room_read_state; this story added focused schema assertions proving fresh rooms/session_navigation/app read-state tables contain no owner_scope or principal_id columns, no owner/principal indexes, and do not create room_members, principal_session_stats, or principal_room_stats. Removed active Chat room/navigation writes to legacy owner columns: ChatRoomService no longer inserts rooms.owner_scope or deletes room_members, and NavigationStore no longer inserts/updates session_navigation.owner_scope. Added explicit temp-DB fixture migration helper migrateLegacyChatDataSchemaToOwnerless in the isolated src/data/final-app-space-cutover-migration.ts path for pibo.sqlite Chat tables: it retires duplicate default room metadata using newest non-archived room selection, rebuilds rooms and session_navigation without owner_scope while preserving IDs and choosing newest duplicate session_navigation rows by session_id, merges principal_session_stats into app_session_read_state by max stream/message cursors and newest read timestamp, merges principal_room_stats into app_room_read_state, drops room_members/principal stats tables, and recreates neutral navigation indexes. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run build; node --test test/data-v2-store.test.mjs test/chat-v2-native-services.test.mjs test/chat-api-routes.test.mjs test/chat-web-shared-sessions.test.mjs; npm run typecheck; and node --test test/data-shared-app-migration.test.mjs. Targeted Docker rg gate over src/data/schema.ts src/data/navigation-store.ts src/apps/chat/data/room-service.ts for fresh owner/principal table/column/index creation patterns returned no matches. Migration fixtures used only temp files in the Docker worker; host /root/.pibo, host Dev/Production gateways, and Production data were not touched. Remaining Chat API/UI ownerScope/principalId payload fields are intentionally left for US-010.

userStories.title: Convert Custom Agents to app-global resources

userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Removed ownerScope from CustomAgentDefinition, CreateCustomAgentInput, UpdateCustomAgentInput-derived payloads, Chat API create normalizers, Chat UI CustomAgent type, and active custom-agent API serialization. CustomAgentStore now creates/list/gets app-global agents without owner input or owner payload fields; Agent Designer and Chat Web handlers call list({ includeArchived }) and create ownerless inputs, and dynamic profile registration loads every active custom agent app-globally. Fresh chat_agents schema remains ownerless and constructor-time temp/historical migration rebuilds old chat_agents tables that still contain owner_scope into ownerless tables. Historical duplicate profile names that were previously separated by owner are resolved deterministically before the rebuild: newest updated row keeps the exact profile_name, older duplicates are renamed to <name>-legacy-<8 hex hash>, with display_name kept in sync. CLI session source no longer derives owner summaries from custom agents. Focused tests assert created/listed/custom-agent API payloads have no ownerScope; temp-file fixtures cover shared/user historical rows, legacy custom-agent profile aliases, duplicate profile-name migration, owner-column drop, and owner-index removal. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run build; node --test test/agent-store.test.mjs test/agent-profiles.test.mjs test/web-channel.test.mjs test/chat-ui-app-agent-catalog-mutations.test.mjs; npm run typecheck. Targeted Docker search found no ownerScope/legacyOwnerScopeForPreCutoverSchemas/shared:app in active custom-agent store/profile/normalizer/UI Agent Designer paths; the only owner_scope match in src/apps/chat/agent-store.ts is the constructor-time historical-column rebuild guard. Tests and migration fixtures used only Docker temp files/fresh test home; host /root/.pibo, host gateways, Production data, deploys, and PR creation were not touched.

acceptanceCriteria: Remove ownerScope from PiboRalphJob, PiboRalphRun, PiboRalphRunFact, and PiboRalphJobCreateInput
Remove ownerScope parameters and getOwnedJob-style methods from Ralph store APIs

userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Removed ownerScope from PiboRalphJob, PiboRalphRun, PiboRalphRunFact, PiboRalphJobCreateInput, and Ralph store/service active contracts. Ralph targets now persist as { kind: "room", roomId } or { kind: "default-chat" }; historical personal/principal targets are normalized to default-chat when rows are read or rebuilt. userStories.title: Create final owner-scope cutover migration inspect and dry-run for isolated homes
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a final migration command that can inspect and dry-run old Owner Scope data inside isolated test homes so that cutover can be reviewed without touching Production.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an operator-only final cutover migration module/command with inspect and dry-run modes
Inspect reports affected files, tables, columns, indexes, row counts, legacy values, and conflict groups without secrets
Dry-run reports planned table rebuilds, merge/rename decisions, and unresolved blockers without writes
The command defaults to an explicit --root or sandbox path and refuses to target /root/.pibo unless a future manual-production flag is added outside this autonomous loop
All inspect/dry-run validation uses Docker worker test homes or temporary fixture databases only
Migration inspect/dry-run fixture tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Added operator-only pibo data final-cutover inspect and dry-run commands backed by src/data/final-app-space-cutover-migration.ts. The command requires an explicit --root or PIBO_MIGRATION_SANDBOX_HOME sandbox, opens known affected SQLite files read-only, and refuses /root/.pibo or paths under it. Inspect reports affected database files, tables, legacy owner/principal columns, owner/principal indexes, row counts, redacted legacy values, and conflict groups without dumping secret content. Dry-run adds planned rebuild/drop/merge/rename/default-chat-normalization actions and unresolved blocker reporting without writes. Conflict detection currently covers duplicate default Chat rooms, duplicate session_navigation rows, duplicate Custom Agent profile names, and legacy Ralph/Cron personal/principal targets. Added focused fixture and CLI tests in test/final-app-space-cutover-migration.test.mjs covering inspect, dry-run, redaction, no-write behavior, CLI JSON output, sandbox/env root requirements, and /root/.pibo refusal. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run build; node --test test/final-app-space-cutover-migration.test.mjs; npm run typecheck. Docker migration sandbox validation ran final-cutover inspect/dry-run against /workspace/.pibo/ralph-migration-sandbox and produced totals: inspect affectedDatabases=6, legacyColumns=19, legacyIndexes=15, legacyRows=4349, conflictGroups=1, plannedActions=0, unresolvedBlockers=0; dry-run same affected totals with plannedActions=20 and unresolvedBlockers=0. All migration inspect/dry-run commands used Docker fixture roots or the Docker migration sandbox only; host /root/.pibo, host gateways, Production data, deploys, apply mode, and PR creation were not touched.

userStories.title: Implement final owner-scope cutover migration apply for Docker test databases only
userStories.description: As an operator, I want transactional apply behavior with backup verification and post-checks that is proven on isolated Docker test databases before any real data cutover is considered.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply mode requires a verified backup path and refuses unresolved conflicts
Apply rebuilds affected SQLite tables without owner/principal columns, membership tables, or owner indexes
Apply is transactional per database file and idempotent on already-migrated schemas
Post-check runs row-count checks and PRAGMA quick_check and writes a migration report
Rollback instructions are generated or documented with the backup path
Apply tests run only against temporary fixture DBs or the Docker migration sandbox, never Production or /root/.pibo
The autonomous Ralph loop must not run apply against the host Production database
Migration apply/idempotency/rollback fixture tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-01. Extended the operator-only pibo data final-cutover command with backup-gated apply mode for isolated Docker fixture/sandbox roots. Apply requires --root plus --backup, refuses /root/.pibo through the existing root guard, requires the backup directory to live outside the target root, verifies matching backup copies for each existing affected SQLite database with PRAGMA quick_check, and refuses unresolved inspect blockers before writes. Apply runs per-database BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions, applies database-specific conflict decisions (Chat duplicate defaults/navigation and principal read-state merge, Custom Agent duplicate profile-name rename, Ralph/Cron personal/principal target normalization), drops membership/principal stats tables, rebuilds remaining tables without owner_scope/principal_id columns, removes owner/principal indexes, runs row-count checks and post-apply PRAGMA quick_check, writes a JSON migration report under migration-reports, and includes rollback instructions referencing the verified backup path. Added fixture coverage for apply, idempotency, missing/invalid backup refusal, host-root refusal, schema cleanup, target normalization, report persistence, row-count/post-check output, and CLI inspect/dry-run/apply JSON paths. Docker validation passed with PIBO_HOME=/workspace/.pibo/ralph-test-home: npm run build; node --test test/final-app-space-cutover-migration.test.mjs; npm run typecheck. Apply validation used only Docker temp fixture homes/backups; no apply was run against host /root/.pibo or Production, and host gateways/deploys/PR creation were not touched.
